title: [CI:DOCS] man pages: document some --format options: images
body:
Baby steps toward merging #14046: document Go format options
for podman images.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
document Go template formats for podman images
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Marking draft because I'm feeling less and less happy with the DADT approach.
@edsantiago Could you remove the Draft if you are ready to merge.
I'm not ready to merge. I want @mheon's and @Luap99's opinion on the format-hiding code. This is totally non-urgent, it can wait a week or two.

I don't really like the extra hiding comment. We can hide stuff in the completion code but the question is how can it know what you and/or others consider not useful? One possibility is to use field tags on structs (like the json tags) so we could have the logic in the podman code. This would at least sync completion and docs.

The real answer to that problem (as stated before) is to create proper structs only for the go templates formatting and stop leaking internal structs in the cli. This requires a lot of work but is IMO the only solution in the long term.

I hate that solution, but I think you're right that it's the direction we have to go. We can't tie our internal data representation to Docker structs, but templates force us to do so for compatibility. The best of our bad options is to have duplicate structs for templates that (mostly) mirror the Docker versions, and convert over to them when necessary, decoupling our internal data representation from our external presentation.

That all went so far over my head that I never even heard the _whoosh_.

I could interpret both your comments as "Ed, just stop working on this". I choose instead to press on because this is important to me both as a QE nerd wanting to prevent future struct regressions, and as a mere user of podman who likes using command completion in daily life. I will document the internal fields with placeholders, and will resubmit this PR, and will submit a future series of PRs until we have everything documented, and will then submit a safety-net consistency-check PR. If that's not the right approach, the simple solution is to block my PRs.

Can we discus this at cabal?

@edsantiago Do you have a list of "unwanted" fields?

Sure, discussion is always welcome. I don't have an actual list, because I'm doing all this through trial and error. Another useless one is `podman events --format '{{.Details....`, all it provides is `.Attributes`, `.ID`, and `.PodID`, all of which are already present in the top level.
Should we just merge this and fix it up in later PRs?

That would be my preference, but keep in mind that I need to follow this up with a dozen or two more PRs, on many more man pages. (Unlike the man-page-refactoring PRs, I don't have a pipeline of these.) The eventual goal is to get placeholders for all options, then enable the cross-checker, and then at least we'll have coverage of new thingies appearing in completion (whether intentional or not).
